item.title: Version 0.1.1
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.1 — quieter idle, snappier feel
This release is mostly performance work. Idle CPU drops to roughly nothing on Apple Silicon — the previous build had a hover-detection timer running 10 times a second whether or not anything was happening, and a search cache rebuilding itself every time any IDE window changed title.
### Performance - Dynamic Island hover monitor halves its polling rate (10 Hz → 5 Hz) and skips hit-tests entirely when the cursor isn't anywhere near the top of the screen. This is the biggest win — most ticks now exit on a single coordinate compare. - Search caches no longer rebuild while the panel is hidden. They refresh automatically the moment you open it, so badges and filter chips are always current. - Global hotkey event tap reads cached shortcut bindings instead of touching the observable settings store on every keystroke.
### Fixes (since 0.1.0) - Dynamic Island flares now meet the bezel with a clean horizontal tangent, and the pill blends into the notch and external-display top edge. - Search result rows no longer drift when the same project appears under multiple paths. - Settings detail pane no longer picks up the accent tint on its form background. - Click-to-pin removed from the Dynamic Island; hover-out collapse is reliable again.
### Known limits - No in-app purchase yet (license server still being stood up). Trial runs unattended. - macOS 26+ only.
Feedback welcome at support@odak.fyi.

item.title: Version 0.1.2
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.2 — fixes a launch crash on second launch
A bug-fix release for a crash that some users hit on Apple Silicon Macs after using the app once: the next launch could trap inside `applicationDidFinishLaunching` and the app would never reach its first window.
### Fixes - Don't crash on launch when two configured scan folders overlap (a parent and one of its subfolders, or roots that resolve to the same target). The project scanner now drops duplicate paths before returning, which kept the Dynamic Island, search, and App Intents from tripping a duplicate-key trap when building their path-keyed lookups.
No behavior or UI changes — same 0.1.1, just no crash.

item.title: Version 0.1.3
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.3 — notch matches the Quick Switcher
The Dynamic Island now shows the same set of open IDE windows that the Quick Switcher does, with one row per actual project.
### Improvements - **DataGrip and other JetBrains-virtual projects** now appear in the notch. Previously only projects living in your configured scan folders showed up, so DataGrip sessions (and any project opened from outside your scan roots) silently went missing even when the Switcher could see them. - **Same-named projects at different paths render as separate rows.** Two `Swee` projects in different folders no longer collapse into a single ambiguous row whose icon belonged to whichever scan-folder iteration order won. - **Each open window gets its own icon in the row's strip.** Two Cursor windows of the same project show as two Cursor icons — each focusable independently — instead of being deduplicated into one.
### Fixes - Hovering one row no longer highlights another. Variable row heights from a missing subtitle line, plus a couple of paths that produced colliding row IDs, were causing multiple rows to light up at once.

item.title: Version 0.1.4
item.description:
## Odak 0.1.4 — same-name projects, everywhere
When two projects share a name (e.g. an `odak.fyi` checkout in `~/code/` and another in `~/code/configs/`), Odak now shows the disambiguating parent folder as a small chip next to the project name on every surface, not just the search results.
### Improvements - **Parent-folder chip in the Quick Switcher and Dynamic Island.** Two windows or rows for same-named projects in different folders now show a `configs` / `code` pill next to the title, the same one you've seen in search. The notch uses a higher-contrast variant so the pill stays readable on the dark notch surface in light mode.
### Fixes - **Xcode-opened projects now match their scanned project correctly.** Opening Odak (or any Xcode project whose inner source directory shares the project name) used to produce two rows in the notch — one for the scanned project, one phantom row labelled like `Odak Odak` — and the search row would lose its Xcode icon. Windows resolved into a subdirectory of a scanned project now fold into that project everywhere.
